decision: choose Arena methodology/historical dataset, SWE-bench, LiveCodeBench, Terminal-Bench, inference-contract matrix, and trust blind-spot matrix as the first wave
rationale: This set teaches preference rankings, repository repair, contamination-aware code tasks, terminal-agent harnesses, reasoning-score contracts, and trust blind spots without relying on live leaderboard crowns.
blocked_claims: live rank; best model; productivity; safety; autonomy; broad intelligence
next_action: Use existing diagrams where possible and only capture source pages when fields are complete.

decision: hold Artificial Analysis index and performance/pricing pages until a dated snapshot pass freezes methodology and rows
rationale: Dynamic aggregate and pricing/performance pages are useful, but they decay quickly and need access time, measurement window, and row-level provenance.
blocked_claims: objective intelligence rank; price-quality frontier; provider recommendation; enterprise ROI
next_action: Create row-level snapshots before any chart or screenshot.

decision: use MMLU, GPQA, MATH, HumanEval, and TruthfulQA as genealogy/source-card surfaces rather than model-crown charts
rationale: These benchmarks matter historically, but their safest exhibit role is to show benchmark lineage and blind spots.
blocked_claims: general intelligence; truth; solved reasoning; production coding ability; current rank
next_action: Route through source-excerpt or genealogy diagram treatment.

decision: prefer the Chapter 19 coding benchmark ladder over multiple separate leaderboard screenshots
rationale: A synthesis ladder teaches how HumanEval, SWE-bench, and LiveCodeBench differ while avoiding noisy screenshot stacks.
blocked_claims: best coding model; productivity; code quality; broad competence
next_action: Keep A-0092 unless final layout needs one source-page inset.

decision: make A-0080 the caption contract for any reasoning, test-time compute, agent, or tool-use score
rationale: Reasoning and agent scores are easy to overread unless the inference budget, scaffold, tools, verifier, and contamination scope are explicit.
blocked_claims: raw-model superiority; all-domain reasoning; autonomy; safety; productivity
next_action: Require the inference-contract fields before any numeric score appears.

decision: use the blind-spot matrix before any truthfulness or safety benchmark page
rationale: Trust exhibits should make measurement limits visible before readers encounter a rank or score.
blocked_claims: truthful model; safe model; hallucination rate; deployed reliability
next_action: Put A-0076 ahead of TruthfulQA source cards in Chapter 23 planning.

decision: every benchmark caption must name benchmark, version/date, model/date if applicable, task scope, scaffold/tools, metric, source, and blocked claims
rationale: Benchmark exhibits are permission devices, not trophies; captions must prevent rank, productivity, safety, and business-value laundering.
blocked_claims: live leaderboard; king; dominates; settled; proves real-world value
next_action: Carry required fields into final captions, source-card specs, and manifest notes.

decision: no new assets_manifest rows in this pass because no screenshot, raster, or final artwork was created
rationale: This pass selects and governs exhibits only; manifest updates belong to later capture/redraw execution.
blocked_claims: publication rights; raster hash; capture date; source-page integrity
next_action: Update assets_manifest.tsv only when a new screenshot or final artwork exists.
